info.title: please
info.description: dotnet-please global tool for streamlining repetitive tasks around Visual Studio projects and solutions.

options.name: workspace
options.description: Specify the solutions or projects to work on. When omitted, the workspace is inferred from the directory hierarchy, resolving a single solution, a project file, or all projects under the current working directory, recursively.

options.name: dry-run
options.description: Don't apply changes, just list what needs to be done

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: add-to-path
commands.description: Adds a directory to the PATH environment variable of the current user.

options.name: self
options.description: Add the directory of DotNetPlease.exe to the PATH variable (and ignore the directory argument)

options.name: workspace
options.description: Specify the solutions or projects to work on. When omitted, the workspace is inferred from the directory hierarchy, resolving a single solution, a project file, or all projects under the current working directory, recursively.

options.name: dry-run
options.description: Don't apply changes, just list what needs to be done

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: Directory
arguments.description: The path to the directory, relative to the working directory (defaults to ".")

commands.name: change-namespace
commands.description: Renames projects matching the specified namespace (does not change code files, use a tool like ReSharper to do that)

options.name: force
options.description: Force delete existing directories

options.name: workspace
options.description: Specify the solutions or projects to work on. When omitted, the workspace is inferred from the directory hierarchy, resolving a single solution, a project file, or all projects under the current working directory, recursively.

options.name: dry-run
options.description: Don't apply changes, just list what needs to be done

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: cleanup-project-files
commands.description: Removes code files from the project directory that are explicitly excluded with a Compile Remove item.

options.name: allow-globs
options.description: Remove all code files that are excluded, even those removed with globs

options.name: workspace
options.description: Specify the solutions or projects to work on. When omitted, the workspace is inferred from the directory hierarchy, resolving a single solution, a project file, or all projects under the current working directory, recursively.

options.name: dry-run
options.description: Don't apply changes, just list what needs to be done

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: consolidate-packages
commands.description: Updates PackageReferences to the highest version found in the solution. Does not actually run any nuget commands.

options.name: props
options.description: The name of an optional .props file where the consolidated versions are saved. If provided, the version numbers in project references are replaced with MSBuild properties

options.name: explicit
options.description: Force explicit version numbers instead of properties

options.name: package
options.description: Limit the command to specific packages. The '*' character can be used as a wildcard.

options.name: version
options.description: Set the package version explicitly. Only used when --package is also set.

options.name: cleanup
options.description: Clean up the props file by removing unused and redundant properties

options.name: force
options.description: Overwrite versions even if they are already defined with an expression

options.name: workspace
options.description: Specify the solutions or projects to work on. When omitted, the workspace is inferred from the directory hierarchy, resolving a single solution, a project file, or all projects under the current working directory, recursively.

options.name: dry-run
options.description: Don't apply changes, just list what needs to be done

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: evaluate-props
commands.description: Evaluates and lists all properties from the selected project(s)

commands.name: expand-references
commands.description: Includes projects from the specified solution or globbing pattern to the current solution, replacing any Reference and PackageReference items

arguments.name: ProjectsOrSolutions

commands.name: find-stray-projects
commands.description: Searches for projects NOT included in a solution.

arguments.name: ProjectName
arguments.description: The original project reference (project name in solution or relative path to project file)

arguments.name: NewProjectName
arguments.description: The new project reference (name or relative path)

commands.name: pull-package-versions
commands.description: Pulls package versions from PackageReference items into PackageVersion items in a centrally managed file

options.name: update
options.description: Update the centrally managed version to the highest one found in PackageReference items (turned off by default)

options.name: workspace
options.description: Specify the solutions or projects to work on. When omitted, the workspace is inferred from the directory hierarchy, resolving a single solution, a project file, or all projects under the current working directory, recursively.

options.description: Don't apply changes, just list what needs to be done

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: PackageVersionsFileName
arguments.description: The file where the PackageVersion items are kept (defaults to Directory.Packages.props)

commands.name: remove-from-path
commands.description: Removes a directory from the PATH environment variable of the current user.

options.name: self
options.description: Remove the directory of DotNetPlease.exe from the PATH variable (and ignore the directory argument)

options.description: Remove bin and obj folders
